TITLE: Home · Monday Music Mix
EYEBROW: Current mix
H1: Thirtysixth
COPY: Also known as the Tuesday music mix. One week and one day later than originally should’ve been out in the wild. Some new, some old and reimagined.
BUTTON: Open mix
BUTTON: Browse archive
EYEBROW: Recent mixes
H2: Small archive, real entries.
LINK: See all
META: September 2013 · Mix 35
H3: Thirtyfifth
META: 3 highlighted tracks · 2 related notes
TEXT: A bit late given this is going up in the last hour of Monday but it still counts, right? Right. Left out a lot of really great music off this mix. Maybe someday I should do a Monday Music Mix: Rejects edition. Soon.
META: August 2013 · Mix 34
H3: Thirtyfourth
META: 4 highlighted tracks · 1 related note
TEXT: New week, new mix. A little birdy from the mountains had to prod me to post this today.
META: July 2013 · Mix 33
H3: Thirtythird
META: 3 highlighted tracks
TEXT: As always, back after a fairly long period of absence. MMM isn’t dead, not just yet.
META: March 2013 · Mix 32
H3: Thirtysecond
META: 3 highlighted tracks
TEXT: Brand new mix at a brand new address. New Postal Service demo, a fantastic AlunaGeorge track and some other surprises.
EYEBROW: From the notebook
H2: Notes that explain why a mix stayed around.
COPY: Short editorial fragments now have their own pages and stay wired back into the archive.

TEXT: Why the Tumblr archive is being normalized into durable JSON files and indexes.
EYEBROW: Connected reading
H2: Notes related to Thirtysixth
COPY: The homepage now exposes the writing closest to the featured mix instead of leaving it stranded on a separate index.
META: April 3, 2026
H3: How the mixes are read
TEXT: A lightweight editorial note on what stands out in the seeded 2013 mixes.
META: April 3, 2026
H3: Rebuilding the archive
TEXT: Why the Tumblr archive is being normalized into durable JSON files and indexes.
EYEBROW: Why it exists
H2: Less brand world, more listening habit.
COPY: A personal archive for mixes built slowly, sequenced by hand, and kept with just enough context to matter later.
COPY: The visual language stays dark, editorial, and grounded: late-night listening, track sequencing, and the useful residue that remains after repeat plays.
LINK: Open the local studio dashboard
